description: Buy SPX calls when synthetic VIX spikes then recedes - bottom fishing
entry_signal: WVF > upper BB then crosses back below
exit_signal: WVF returns to normal range or time-based
option_type: Call debit spread or naked calls
related_strategy: Pure price-derived. No VIX data needed. Test immediately.

description: Pause all credit spread strategies when WVF > upper BB
related_strategy: Universal kill switch based on synthetic fear.

description: Only enter bullish trades when VIX < SMA(5) - fear subsiding
related_strategy: Simple and clean. Test as additive filter.

description: Set short strikes beyond VIX-implied daily range
related_strategy: Statistically grounded strike selection. High priority.

description: Sell credit spreads when SPX hits VIX-implied daily boundary
entry_signal: SPX touches upper boundary = sell call spread. Lower = sell put spread
related_strategy: Quantitative version of Zenith exhaustion concept.

description: Only trade credit spreads in contango. Halt in backwardation
related_strategy: Priority 1. Prevents trading during fear spikes. VIX9D/VIX ratio available now.

description: Use VVIX spike as proxy for VIX option panic - halt all strategies
related_strategy: Addresses MEDS tail risk directly. VVIX is our proxy for VIX option behavior.

description: Use Hull MA on 5min SPX as low-lag trend filter
entry_signal: HMA(20) on 5min trending up = sell puts. Down = sell calls
related_strategy: Lower lag than EMA. Less whipsaw. Test as MEDS EMA replacement.

description: Replace Apex fixed d=12 with VIX-implied distance. High VIX = wider d. Low VIX = tighter d for more premium.
entry_signal: At 15:55 compute expected_remaining_move = SPX*(VIX/15.87/100)*sqrt(5/390). Set d = 2x expected move.
notes: Apex uses fixed d=12. VIX-adaptive d could capture more premium on calm days and protect on volatile days. Key: sqrt(5/390) scales from daily to 5-min remaining.

description: Skip Apex trades when VIX9D/VIX > 1.0 (backwardation). Apex has 100% WR so any filter must not kill too many trades.
entry_signal: Check VIX9D/VIX at 15:50. If > 1.0 skip day.
notes: Apex already skips Wed+Thu. Adding term structure check could prevent the rare loss day that hasn't appeared yet. Conservative: only skip when ratio > 1.05.

description: Compute Williams VIX Fix on intraday SPX bars. If WVF is elevated at 15:50 skip entry - market still in panic mode.
entry_signal: Compute WVF on 5-min SPX bars. If WVF(15:50) > upper BB skip.
notes: Purely price-derived. No external data. Would catch intraday capitulation that prior-day filter misses.

description: Instead of always both-sides use VIX16 range position to decide. If SPX at 15:55 is near upper expected boundary sell calls only (overextended up). Near lower sell puts only.
entry_signal: At 15:55: pos_in_range = (SPX-lower)/(upper-lower). If >0.7 = call-only. If <0.3 = put-only. Else both.
notes: Apex both-sides is great but single-side at extremes may capture more edge. Worth testing.

description: Enhance LastFive VIX-adaptive distance using VIX9D instead of VIX. VIX9D better captures near-term (9-day) fear which is more relevant for 0DTE.
entry_signal: Replace VIX thresholds with VIX9D: VIX9D<16 -> d=0 (ATM). 16-20 -> d=3. 20-24 -> d=5. >24 -> skip.
notes: VIX9D is theoretically better for 0DTE than VIX(30-day). We have VIX9D data. Direct upgrade path.

description: Add Williams VIX Fix as additional put-side filter. If WVF elevated at 15:50 skip PUT side (market dropping sharply).
entry_signal: Compute daily WVF. If WVF > percentile(85) skip PUT entries.
notes: LastFive already has put momentum filter. WVF adds a different angle - captures multi-day capitulation not just afternoon momentum.

description: Only trade LastFive when VIX term structure is in contango. Backwardation = skip entirely.
entry_signal: Check VIX9D/VIX at 15:50. If > 1.0 skip all trades. If between 0.9-1.0 reduce sizing by 50%.
notes: LastFive already skips VIX>26 days. Term structure adds regime awareness. Could reduce DD from -$5.6k.

description: Use VIX/16 to compute expected remaining move in last 5 min. Set distance = 1.5x expected remaining move for better strike calibration.
entry_signal: expected_5min_move = SPX * (VIX/15.87/100) * sqrt(5/390). Set dist = ceil(1.5 * move / 5) * 5.
notes: Current VIX-adaptive uses fixed buckets. VIX16 gives continuous calibration. More precise especially in VIX 18-22 zone where current buckets are coarse.

description: Scale the protective buyback trigger (currently $2 SPX move) by VIX-implied 1-min expected move. High VIX = wider trigger to avoid false buybacks.
entry_signal: expected_1min = SPX*(VIX/15.87/100)*sqrt(1/390). Buyback if SPX moves > 3x expected_1min toward strike.
notes: Fixed $2 trigger may be too tight on high-VIX days (causing unnecessary buybacks) and too loose on low-VIX days.
